model_narrative: We are scheduling a project made up of several jobs. For each job we decide when it starts and how many days it takes to finish. A job can be completed faster or slower within an allowed range, and its cost depends on the duration we pick, running from a known cost at the fastest allowed duration to a known cost at the slowest allowed duration. The objective is to choose start days and durations so that the total cost of completing all the jobs is as small as possible.

sets.name: J
sets.doc: the jobs that make up the project, each of which must be scheduled

sets.name: P
sets.doc: the precedence pairs of jobs; each member is an ordered pair in which the first job is a predecessor that must finish before the second job is allowed to begin

params.name: mincost
params.doc: the cost incurred for completing a job in its minimum allowed duration, in dollars per job

params.name: maxcost
params.doc: the cost incurred for completing a job in its maximum allowed duration, in dollars per job

params.name: mintime
params.doc: the minimum number of days a job is permitted to take

params.name: maxtime
params.doc: the maximum number of days a job is permitted to take

params.name: totaldays
params.doc: the total number of days available to complete the entire project

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the start day chosen for each job, a positive integer

vars.name: t
vars.doc: the duration in days chosen for each job, a positive integer

constraints.name: t[j]
constraints.intent: Every job has a shortest duration it is allowed to take. For each job, the duration chosen must be at least that job's minimum allowed duration.

constraints.name: t[j]
constraints.intent: Every job has a longest duration it is allowed to take. For each job, the duration chosen must not exceed that job's maximum allowed duration.

constraints.name: s[j]
constraints.intent: The whole project must fit inside the available time window. For each job, the day it starts plus the number of days it takes must not run past the total number of days available for the project.

constraints.name: s[i]
constraints.intent: Some jobs cannot begin until other jobs are finished. For each precedence pair, the predecessor job must be completely finished before the dependent job is allowed to start, meaning the predecessor's start day plus its duration must come no later than the start day of the job that depends on it.

query: There's a proposal on the table to leave a 2-day buffer at the end, so Job 7 would have to wrap up on or before day 38 rather than running right to the deadline. Plug that in and tell me the resulting cost.

query: We're looking at inserting a 2-day inspection gap between Job 3 finishing and Job 5 beginning, so Job 5's start would have to be at least 2 days after Job 3 ends. What does the schedule cost with that handoff gap in place?

query: In the plan Job 3 starts the instant Job 2 wraps, with no breathing room for handoff. Is there a reason we couldn't leave at least a one-day gap between Job 2 finishing and Job 3 starting? What's pulling the model the other way?
